medical_history.history_of_present_illness: ___ y/o female with a history of chronic migraine, h/o TIA, and cervical spasm who developed worse than usual headache. Since her teenage years patient has had frequent migraines, often daily. 6 weeks PTA patient fell and hit her head at ___, since that time patient has had worse symptoms from her chronic daily migraines. On ___ patient underwent facet injection at C2-C3 at OSH. This caused further exacerbation of her headaches. On ___ she was NPO after midnight for a GI motility study. On ___ (morning of admission) she developed nausea and subsequently began dry heaving (with one episode of emesis) which worsened her headache pain. She describes the headache as throbbing, constant and diffuse. It is worse with standing and better with lying flat. She has associated pressure behind the right eye and describes a white orb floating in front of the eye. She denies any fevers or chills but does report some sensitivity to light.
